hazard.summary: Provider code_search rate-limit headers are ignored across the API transport to skill-search orchestration boundary — parallel fanout exhausts a narrow resource-specific quota while the client plans against the broader account limit, causing workflow stall after 1-2 operations.
signals: Run a second search for a different skill name
GitHub API rate limit exceeded. Please wait a minute and try again.
I suspect what's actually happening here is that you're hitting the code_search rate limit of 10 per minute when running skill search (which kicks off a few parallel requests).
{"message": "API rate limit exceeded for user ID 1611510.", "status": "403"}

constraints: Clients must budget API operations against provider resource-specific quotas when provider emits resource-scoped rate-limit headers.
Clients must not plan capacity from broad account limits when the invoked endpoint consumes a narrower resource-specific quota.
Rate-limit response headers must propagate into orchestration and retry decisions before additional same-resource requests are launched.
Same-resource request fanout must be bounded by remaining resource quota and reset time.
Retry guidance must use provider reset or retry signals for the exhausted resource rather than a generic delay.
Interactive discovery workflows must throttle, cache, batch, or reduce requests before exhausting narrow provider quotas.
knobs: per-resource token bucket keyed by X-Ratelimit-Resource
maximum in-flight /search/code requests per command invocation
verification: Setup → Configure a GitHub API test double so /search/code responses return X-Ratelimit-Resource: code_search and X-Ratelimit-Remaining: 0. Action → Run GH_DEBUG=api gh skill search agent-browser --limit 5. Assertion → The client does not launch additional /search/code requests after observing the exhausted code_search budget and reports reset-aware throttling.

Setup → Configure /search/code with a resource-specific quota lower than the general authenticated API quota. Action → Run two different gh skill search commands back-to-back. Assertion → The second command uses cached results, throttles, or waits according to the code_search resource budget and avoids an immediate HTTP/2.0 403 Forbidden.

Setup → Capture GH_DEBUG=api output for gh skill search with multiple generated code search queries. Action → Execute gh skill search agent-browser --limit 5. Assertion → The number of concurrent GET /search/code requests is bounded by the configured concurrency knob and does not exceed available X-Ratelimit-Remaining.

Setup → Mock a 403 response containing X-Ratelimit-Resource: code_search, X-Ratelimit-Remaining: 0, and X-Ratelimit-Reset: 1777364425. Action → Invoke the skill discovery command. Assertion → The user-facing message identifies the code_search resource limit and uses the reset/backoff signal rather than a generic delay.
notes: Dominant causal chain: GitHub API emits HTTP 403 plus resource-specific headers for code_search quota exhaustion → headers propagate through API transport and are visible in GH_DEBUG=api → gh skill search orchestration has already kicked off multiple parallel /search/code requests and does not budget against the code_search resource → resource-specific exhaustion signal is ignored at the transport-to-orchestrator boundary → the client skips throttling/backoff/query reduction based on X-Ratelimit-Resource, X-Ratelimit-Remaining, and X-Ratelimit-Reset → skill discovery becomes unusable after 1-2 operations. Note: GitHub uses HTTP 403 for code_search quota exhaustion with full rate-limit headers — functionally equivalent to 429 for classification purposes.

constraints: Clients must budget API operations against resource-specific provider quotas.
Clients must not launch parallel same-resource requests without checking remaining quota.
Clients must propagate rate-limit headers into retry and orchestration decisions.
Clients must honor reset-aware backoff for exhausted resources.
anti_patterns: planning against the general authenticated API limit while consuming a narrower endpoint-specific quota
kicking off parallel code search requests before checking code_search remaining budget
showing a generic delay when provider reset headers are available
remote discovery that depends on repeated uncached provider search calls
